ground_truth.title: Global Language Learning Trends and Statistics
ground_truth.main_content: As the world becomes more interconnected, global language learning initiatives take on an increased importance, as language barriers can pose problems for technology, trade, commerce, travel, and more. Students who are interested in pursuing degrees in foreign languages and entering diverse careers need a comprehensive picture of foreign language education trends, emerging technologies, and the many pathways available in the language acquisition space. Let's take a look at some key language learning statistics and trends so you can be better prepared for your career. Growth of Language Learning Worldwide: More than 1.5 billion people around the world are actively studying a foreign language. More than 7,000 languages are spoken throughout the world today. The global language learning market's valuation was calculated at $70.69 billion in 2022, but has been projected to grow to $187.69 billion by 2028. Digital learning initiatives specifically are projected to grow $14 billion from 2022 to 2026. Most Popular Languages for Learning: English is the most studied language in the world, with approximately 1.5 billion language learners and speakers spread throughout 135+ countries. Spanish is another prominent global language, with roughly 500 million speakers. French claims 300 million speakers, while German has over 100 million speakers. Foreign Language Education Trends in Technology: Mobile apps, speech recognition and analysis programs, AI-powered tools, AR or augmented reality tools, and teaching programs like Babbel and Duolingo are revolutionizing language learning. The Impact of COVID-19: In 2020 the global online language learning market grew approximately 38%. Regional Variations and Cultural Influences: In continental Europe, approximately 96% of students study English as a foreign language, and more than 59% of Europeans speak at least one foreign language. In the U.S., only 20-30% of the population is bilingual, with 18% of Americans reporting regularly speaking their second language. Challenges Facing Global Language Learning Initiatives: 40% of people worldwide do not have proper access to education in the languages they themselves speak and understand, and this percentage is closer to 90% for some low- and middle-income countries. One language disappears every two weeks.
